questions.question: What structural capability advantage does a Callable task possess compared to a standard Runnable task?
answers: A Callable can return a computed result value and throw checked exceptions
A Callable does not require module-info reflection opens configurations
A Callable runs directly inside kernel privilege rings bypassing restrictions
A Callable can be restarted multiple times within the same thread lifecycle

questions.question: What thread safety violation occurs when a thread caches local variable copies instead of reading from main RAM?
answers: Memory Visibility Problem
Race Condition anomaly
Deadlock layout lock
Illegal Thread State condition

questions.question: Which thread-safe collection from java.util.concurrent should replace a standard HashMap in high-concurrency environments?
answers: Hashtable
Collections.synchronizedMap
LinkedHashMap module structure
ConcurrentHashMap

questions.question: What behavioral trait separates a TreeMap from a standard HashMap implementation?
answers: TreeMap is limited to storing integer primitive values
HashMap encrypts key fields; TreeMap leaves characters visible
TreeMap prevents asynchronous thread execution steps from accessing data
TreeMap maintains its keys sorted according to their natural ordering or a custom Comparator

questions.question: Which interface inside the Java Collections Framework guarantees that no duplicate elements can be stored?
answers: Set
Queue
List
Map entries tracker

questions.question: What performance penalty can hit a HashMap if multiple distinct keys yield identical hashCode values?
answers: The database loader class drops its connection stream pipe instantly
The JVM throws a ConcurrentModificationException and aborts the thread
Hash collisions increase, causing lookup speeds to degrade from O(1) toward O(N)
The internal memory mapping flushes all cached key allocations

questions.question: Why are primitive types like int or char forbidden from being passed directly as parameters to Java Generics?
answers: Primitives consume too much L1 cache memory causing thread scheduler stalls
Database drivers cannot bind primitive types to statement query parameters
Generics require object references on the heap; primitives do not inherit from Object
The modularity guidelines written in module-info block primitive parameters

questions.question: What does the wildcard bound pattern 'List<? extends Number>' accomplish inside a method signature?
answers: It mandates that all elements in the List must carry matching static values
It opens the List to accept additions of any custom object structure
It turns the List into an immutable collection
It restricts the parameter to a List containing Number or any of its subclasses

questions.question: What does the wildcard pattern 'List<? super Integer>' signify inside type boundaries checking?
answers: It specifies a List that can hold Integer or any of its master superclass ancestor layers
It prevents downstream functions from executing list mutations
It tells the system that the collection properties are bound to an index
It limits the collection strictly to elements that implement serializable

questions.question: What happens when you use a raw type instead of a parameterized generic type declaration?
answers: You lose compile-time type safety checking benefits and trigger compiler warning logs
Jackson's ObjectMapper becomes unable to parse JSON resource file streams
The application crashes instantly with a ClassCastException upon boot paths
The Java Virtual Machine executes the bytecode lines 50 percent slower
